assumptions: No prior Mailgun account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.

first_success.outcome: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.
first_success.observable_signal: The API returns a queued message response and the authorized recipient receives the email.

route_selection.surface: Quickstart: sandbox domain cURL send first email
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Quickstart: sandbox domain cURL send first email

candidates.name: Quickstart: sandbox domain cURL send first email
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Mailgun and open the sandbox-domain quick start.
required_path.observable_result: The sandbox domain and sending credentials are visible.

required_path.action: Authorize and verify the intended sandbox recipient if it is not already authorized.
required_path.observable_result: The recipient is listed as authorized.

required_path.action: Run the documented cURL message send against the sandbox domain using API authentication.
required_path.observable_result: Mailgun returns a successful queued response.

required_path.action: Inspect the authorized recipient inbox and confirm delivery of the first message.
required_path.observable_result: The sent message is visible in the inbox.

prerequisites.description: A Mailgun account with its sandbox domain.
The Mailgun API key shown for the account.
A recipient address authorized for the sandbox domain.

external_gates.description: Sandbox domains send only to authorized recipients, who may need to complete email verification.
Email delivery can be delayed or filtered by the recipient provider.

platform_outcomes.description: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Send Your First Email
evidence_supported: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.
The API returns a queued message response and the authorized recipient receives the email.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Send Your First Email path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
